question.text: What additional certification method is available to Principals under SEBI (CAPSM) Regulations that is NOT available to other associated persons?

answer.text: Delivering at least four sessions in a specific CPE program as specified by NISM
Submitting a self-certification declaration to SEBI
Obtaining a recommendation from two existing certificate holders
Completing an online assessment without appearing for a formal examination

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Principals have three certification methods: (a) passing the exam, (b) completing a CPE program, or (c) delivering at least four sessions in a specific CPE program. This third option — teaching in CPE programs — is available only to Principals, not to other associated persons. **The Trap:** The teaching option recognizes that senior management (sole proprietors, managing partners, whole-time directors) may demonstrate expertise through teaching rather than test-taking. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Persons over 50 or with 10+ years' experience get two options (exam or CPE), while all others must pass the exam. Know the three-tier certification structure.
